texto: But when ye come, and all the flowers are dying,If I am dead, as dead I well may be,You'll come and find the place where I am lying,And kneel and say Ave there for me,And I shall hear, though soft you tread above me,And all my grave will warmer, sweeter be,For you will bend and tell me that you love me,And I shall sleep in peace until you come to me
explicacao: Citação de Frederick Weatherly sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “But when ye come, and all the flowers are dying,If I am dead, as dead I well may be,You'll come a…” articula amor, danny-boy, death em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e termino.
